threats.title: Protocol Insolvency via Uncapped TGT Deposits
threats.hypothesis_description: The MergeTgt contract fails to enforce a maximum cap on TGT deposits. Because the total circulating supply of TGT exceeds the intended exchange quota, attackers can deposit excess TGT, causing the allocated TITN (`totalTitnClaimable`) to exceed the contract's actual TITN balance, leading to insolvency.
threats.investigation_rationale: If the allocated claims exceed the physical token balance, the contract operates as a fractional reserve. Early claimers will successfully withdraw TITN, while late claimers will face transaction reverts, resulting in a bank run scenario.
deployment_assumptions: Total circulating supply of TGT exceeds TGT_TO_EXCHANGE (579,000,000 TGT)
threats.config_evidence: MergeTgt.sol defines TGT_TO_EXCHANGE as '57.9% of MAX_TGT', explicitly confirming that 42.1% more TGT exists outside the intended exchange quota.

proof_of_signal.pattern: totalTitnClaimable is incremented without checking if it exceeds titn.balanceOf(address(this)) or TITN_ARB

preconditions: Attacker has access to TGT tokens on the open market
MergeTgt lockedStatus is OneWay or TwoWay
launchTime is set and < 360 days have passed

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Attacker deposits excess TGT, inflating totalTitnClaimable beyond TITN_ARB
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attacker claims a share of the fixed TITN pool, draining funds meant for legitimate early depositors

economic_questions: What is the current market price of TGT vs TITN?
Is it profitable to buy excess TGT specifically to drain the TITN pool?
validation_requirements: Verify the actual circulating supply of TGT on Arbitrum
Calculate the exact point of insolvency based on current deposits

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Cost of acquiring TGT on the open market
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Up to the entire remaining TITN balance in the contract
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Arbitrage spread between TGT acquisition cost and TITN value

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Before legitimate users claim their TITN
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Drains protocol liquidity, rendering legitimate claims worthless

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: Legitimate TGT depositors who have not yet claimed their TITN
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: 100% loss of expected TITN for late claimers

threats.title: Cross-Chain Tainting Attack Freezes Victim Portfolios
threats.hypothesis_description: The Titn contract permanently flags any recipient of a cross-chain transfer as a bridged token holder. An attacker can bridge a dust amount of TITN to any user on Base, permanently freezing the victim's entire natively-minted TITN balance due to transfer restrictions.
threats.investigation_rationale: This creates a massive griefing vector where a competitor or malicious actor can systematically lock up the liquidity of major token holders, DEX pairs, or protocol integrations on the destination chain for pennies.

proof_of_signal.pattern: isBridgedTokenHolder[_to] = true is set unconditionally on receipt of any bridged amount

preconditions: isBridgedTokensTransferLocked is true on the destination chain (Base)
Victim holds natively minted TITN on the destination chain
Attacker has a minimal amount of TITN on the source chain (Arbitrum)

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: isBridgedTokenHolder flag applies to the entire address, not just the bridged tokens
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Attacker calls send() with 1 wei of TITN targeting the victim's address
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: No direct profit, but enables massive economic griefing, extortion, or competitive sabotage

economic_questions: How cheaply can an attacker taint the top 100 holders on Base?
Can this be used to freeze DEX liquidity pools if they are targeted?
validation_requirements: Test bridging 1 wei to an address with native tokens and verify transfer reverts
Calculate total cost of a mass-tainting attack

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: 1 wei of TITN per victim + LayerZero fees
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_execution_cost: ~$1-2 per victim in cross-chain gas fees

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Any time while isBridgedTokensTransferLocked is true
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Could completely freeze secondary market trading on Base

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: Native TITN holders on Base, potentially including DEX pools
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: 100% loss of token liquidity (tokens cannot be sold or moved)
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Entire circulating supply of native TITN on Base

threats.title: Permanent Lock of Post-Year Claims via Snapshot Underflow
threats.hypothesis_description: If the contract's TITN balance falls below the total allocated claims (due to the uncapped deposits in ECON-H001 or admin withdrawals), the post-1-year withdrawal function will suffer an arithmetic underflow, permanently reverting and locking all remaining user funds.
threats.investigation_rationale: The transition from `claimTitn` to `withdrawRemainingTitn` at 360 days is a hard cutover. If `withdrawRemainingTitn` reverts, users have no alternative path to claim their funds.

proof_of_signal.pattern: uint256 unclaimedTitn = remainingTitnAfter1Year - initialTotalClaimable; (underflows if balance < claims)

preconditions: Contract TITN balance is less than totalTitnClaimable

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Triggered naturally if deposits exceed cap, or if admin withdraws any TITN
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: No profit to attacker, but causes complete loss of funds for all remaining users

economic_questions: What is the likelihood of totalTitnClaimable exceeding the balance before 360 days?
validation_requirements: Simulate the 360-day transition with balance < claims and verify permanent DoS

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Destroys protocol credibility and locks millions of tokens

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All users who waited 360 days to claim their proportional bonus
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All remaining unclaimed TITN in the contract

threats.title: Transfer Exemption Trap Causes Permanent Token Loss
threats.hypothesis_description: The Titn contract explicitly exempts transfers to the `lzEndpoint` from its strict transfer restrictions. This misleads users into attempting to bridge tokens by transferring them directly to the endpoint address, which permanently traps the tokens since actual bridging requires calling the `send()` function.
threats.investigation_rationale: While technically a user error, the contract code explicitly creates a honeypot by allowing this specific, destructive action while blocking safe transfers. The exemption is dead code for legitimate bridging (which uses `_burn`), serving only to facilitate token loss.

proof_of_signal.pattern: to != lzEndpoint exemption in _validateTransfer allows direct ERC20 transfers to the endpoint

preconditions: User is on Arbitrum and wants to bridge TITN to Base
User attempts to use standard ERC20 transfer instead of OFT send()

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Exemption logic allows transfer to endpoint, but endpoint doesn't auto-bridge ERC20 transfers
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: User error induced by misleading contract rules
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Tokens permanently locked in endpoint contract, reducing circulating supply

economic_questions: How many users are likely to attempt direct transfers based on block explorer analysis?
validation_requirements: Verify that LayerZero endpoint does not have a recovery mechanism for accidental ERC20 transfers

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Deflationary pressure due to lost tokens

threats.title: Zero-Yield Deposit Trap at Exact 360-Day Boundary
threats.hypothesis_description: Due to mismatched comparison operators, a user who deposits TGT exactly at the 360-day boundary will have their transaction accepted but will receive 0 claimable TITN, resulting in a complete loss of their deposited TGT.
threats.investigation_rationale: onTokenTransfer allows deposits if time <= 360 days (using `> 360 days` to revert), but quoteTitn only calculates a non-zero return if time < 360 days. At exactly 360 days, the deposit succeeds but yields 0.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Mismatched boundary checks: > 360 days vs < 360 days

preconditions: User deposits TGT in the exact block where block.timestamp - launchTime == 360 days

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: quoteTitn falls through to else block returning 0, while onTokenTransfer proceeds
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: User deposits at the exact boundary timestamp
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Protocol absorbs TGT without issuing TITN liability

economic_questions: Is there any slippage protection or minimum output check in the TGT transferAndCall implementation?
validation_requirements: Simulate deposit at exact 360-day timestamp and verify 0 output

potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Amount deposited in that specific block

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Verify quoteTitn return value at boundary

threats.title: Admin Rug Pull via Unrestricted Withdraw
threats.hypothesis_description: The owner can call the `withdraw` function at any time to extract all TITN tokens from the contract, instantly rendering the protocol insolvent and stealing all user funds.
threats.investigation_rationale: While admin privileges are common, the ability to withdraw the exact tokens allocated to users (TITN) without any timelock or conditional checks violates the core economic invariant of the protocol.

proof_of_signal.pattern: token.safeTransfer(owner(), amount) with no restrictions on token type or amount

preconditions: Admin is malicious or private key is compromised

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Unrestricted transfer of any ERC20 balance to owner
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Admin steals 100% of the protocol's TVL

economic_questions: Is there any off-chain multi-sig or timelock protecting the owner role?
validation_requirements: Verify that withdraw() can drain the TITN token specifically
